prompt: Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Momentum unit standard. Teach these exact points: Momentum unit standard: The standard metric unit for momentum is kg•m/s. Key detail: The standard metric unit for momentum is kg•m/s. Key detail: The standard metric unit of momentum is kg•m/s, but other units like kg•mi/hr, kg•km/hr, and g•cm/s are also acceptable. Use simple labels, large readable text, limited motion, and clear visual cause-and-effect. Avoid dense paragraphs. Include one calm recap frame at the end. Adaptation needs: OpenDyslexic, chunk_size=2, low_text, max_180s, micro_chunks, break_cards, stealth_assessment.

Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Quantity of motion and mass. Teach these exact points: Quantity of motion and mass: Momentum is a physical quantity that includes both mass and velocity, characterizing the "quantity of motion" of an object. Key detail: The momentum (p) of an object is the product of its mass (m) and its velocity (v), represented by the formula p = mv. Key detail: The momentum of an object is equal to the mass of the object times the velocity of the object (p = m • v). Use simple labels, large readable text, limited motion, and clear visual cause-and-effect. Avoid dense paragraphs. Include one calm recap frame at the end. concept: Momentum relationship with mass and velocity
prompt: Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Momentum relationship with mass and velocity. Teach these exact points: Momentum relationship with mass and velocity: A doubling of the mass results in a doubling of the momentum when velocity is constant. Key detail: A quadrupling in velocity results in a quadrupling of the momentum when mass is constant.
